message:
feat(termwiz): track upload region and cell counts in RenderTelemetry

RenderTelemetry now includes two new fields that distinguish between
dirty regions detected by the diff engine and regions actually selected
for upload to the terminal:
- frame_upload_regions: count of dirty regions chosen for upload in the
  active render mode (rect-based, tile-based, or full-screen).
- frame_upload_cells: total dirty cells within those upload regions.

In the current implementation these mirror the existing dirty_rects or
dirty_tiles counters (depending on render mode), but the explicit
separation prepares for partial-upload optimizations where the renderer
may skip uploading regions that fall outside the visible viewport or
are occluded by overlapping surfaces.

Updated all three render telemetry tests (full render, rect-based dirty
tracking, tile-based dirty tracking) to assert the new fields match
their corresponding dirty counters and drop to zero on clean frames.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
